zotero.abstractNote: Audio-visual learning aims to learn representations that capture the correspondence between audio and visual signals. Existing methods typically learn a joint embedding space by contrasting positive audio-visual pairs against negative ones. However, they often ignore the compositional nature of audio-visual events, where an event is composed of multiple objects and sounds. In this paper, we propose a novel framework, Compositional Contrastive Learning (CCL), to distill audio-visual knowledge by explicitly modeling the compositional structure of audio-visual events. Specifically, we introduce a compositional contrastive loss that encourages the model to learn representations that are invariant to the composition of audio and visual components. We further propose a cross-modal distillation strategy to transfer knowledge from a pre-trained audio-visual model to a student model. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on various audio-visual tasks, including audio-visual retrieval, audio-visual classification, and audio-visual event localization.
zotero.conferenceName: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
